content.heading: Callbacks — passing a function as an argument

blocks.text: A callback is a function you pass into another function to be called later. You've already used them — forEach takes a callback and runs it for each item. setTimeout takes a callback and runs it after a delay. Understanding callbacks is the key to understanding how Simon Says controls timing.

blocks.desc: Pass an anonymous function to setTimeout. It runs after 1500ms. The rest of your code keeps running immediately.
Pass a function to forEach. It runs once for each item in the array, with that item passed in as the argument.

lines: // A named function used as a callback
// Pass it to setTimeout — no () here, just the name
// Or pass an anonymous function directly

blocks.label: No () when passing a function
blocks.text: When you pass a function as a callback, do NOT write the () after its name. setTimeout(turnOff, 1500) passes the function. setTimeout(turnOff(), 1500) calls it immediately and passes the result — not what you want.

exercises.title: Predict the order
tasks: Before running — write down the exact order you expect the messages to appear.
Why does 'C' appear before 'B' even though 'B' is written first?
exercises.hint: setTimeout doesn't pause the code. Everything after it runs first, then the callback fires later.

exercises.title: Write a delayed action
tasks: Write a function called lightUp() that logs 'Squares are lit!'.
Write a function called turnOff() that logs 'Squares are off.'
Use setTimeout to call turnOff() 2 seconds later.
This is the exact pattern Simon Says uses for its display phase.
exercises.hint: Pass turnOff to setTimeout without the (): setTimeout(turnOff, 2000)

checks.label: lightUp() exists and can be called
turnOff() exists and can be called

challenges.title: The callback fires immediately instead of after 2 seconds
challenges.description: The turnOff function should run after 2 seconds, but it fires immediately. One character is wrong.
challenges.hint: When you write turnOff() you are calling the function. When you write turnOff you are passing it.
challenges.explanation: setTimeout(turnOff(), 2000) calls turnOff immediately and passes its return value (undefined) to setTimeout. Remove the parentheses: setTimeout(turnOff, 2000). Now you're passing the function itself to be called later.

challenges.title: forEach is not lighting up the right cells
challenges.description: The loop should log 'Lighting up cell X' for each cell in the array. But it's logging undefined for each one. Find the bug.
challenges.hint: The callback function needs a parameter to receive each item from the array.
challenges.explanation: The callback function has no parameter declared — so 'cell' inside it is undefined. Fix: add the parameter: function(cell) { ... }. Now forEach passes each array item into 'cell' on each iteration.
